Performs a contraindication check before prescribing (e.g., pregnancy risk in adolescents, significant hepatic disease, significant renal impairment for NSAIDs, active serious infection, significant cytopenias) and modifies the plan accordingly (critical safety behavior).

Provides key methotrexate safety counseling: once-weekly dosing, avoidance of duplicate daily dosing errors, need for contraception counseling when applicable, and avoidance of alcohol/excess hepatotoxic exposures.
Includes monitoring and risk discussion appropriate to methotrexate/NSAIDs (e.g., baseline and periodic CBC and liver enzymes for methotrexate; renal/GI risk for NSAIDs) without inventing results.
Uses shared decision-making: elicits patient/caregiver goals and concerns, checks understanding, and invites questions.

Avoids inappropriate therapies for this context (e.g., routine antibiotics, opioids for chronic pain without clear indication, systemic steroids as default long-term therapy) unless clearly justified.

minimal_paths.description: Standard L2 treatment initiation for LACC1-associated juvenile idiopathic arthritis with NSAID + methotrexate after required safety checks and counseling.
must_collect: history of liver disease or abnormal liver tests
history of kidney disease or GI ulcer/bleeding
infection symptoms or recent serious infection
baseline symptom severity and functional impact
steps: Explain that the condition is juvenile idiopathic arthritis and that the LACC1 defect can predispose to inflammatory arthritis.
Confirm allergies, current medications/supplements, and screen for contraindications (renal/hepatic disease, GI bleeding risk, pregnancy, active infection).
Record diagnosis as juvenile idiopathic arthritis associated with LACC1 defect.
Start NSAID therapy for pain/inflammation with counseling on GI/renal risks and when to stop/seek care.
Start methotrexate as a DMARD with once-weekly dosing instructions and safety counseling; discuss need for monitoring labs and infection precautions.
Provide return precautions (worsening pain/swelling, fever, GI bleeding, severe abdominal pain, dyspnea, signs of infection) and set expectations for response time to methotrexate.

acceptable_variants.description: NSAID initiation with methotrexate planned/initiated after confirming baseline monitoring arrangements; still completes required safety checks before prescribing.
steps: Explain juvenile idiopathic arthritis diagnosis and genetic association (LACC1).
Complete allergy, interaction, and contraindication screening.
Record diagnosis.
Prescribe NSAID for symptom control with counseling.
Prescribe methotrexate with explicit once-weekly dosing and monitoring plan (baseline and periodic CBC/LFTs) and contraception counseling when applicable.

acceptable_variants.description: Methotrexate initiated with subcutaneous route preference due to GI intolerance risk or adherence considerations, while still including NSAIDs for symptomatic relief.
steps: Explain diagnosis using "juvenile idiopathic arthritis" and relate to LACC1 defect.
Check allergies, interactions, and contraindications.
Record diagnosis.
Prescribe NSAID (e.g., naproxen/ibuprofen) with safety counseling.
Prescribe methotrexate with subcutaneous option and once-weekly dosing emphasis; outline monitoring and infection precautions.
